items.title: U.S. special forces soldier pleads not guilty to using classified information to win big on prediction market
items.summary_auto: A U.S. special forces soldier pleaded not guilty Tuesday to charges that he used classified information about the mission to capture former Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro to win more than $400,000 US on the prediction market Polymarket.

items.title: U.S. Justice Department files motion that some legal critics say reads like a Trump social media post
items.summary_auto: The U.S. Justice Department has asked a federal judge to throw out a lawsuit that has impeded President Donald Trump's plans for a White House ballroom, saying its opponents "suffer from Trump derangement syndrome" and that an alleged assassination attempt on the president showed the ‌project was necessary.

items.title: CFL to open 2027 season in May before adopting new playoff format later that year
items.summary_auto: The Canadian Football League will kick off its season earlier and revamp its playoff format in 2027.

items.title: Former Fauci adviser indicted for allegedly concealing communications related to COVID-19 research
items.summary_auto: Dr. David Morens, 78, is accused of using his private email account to intentionally circumvent public records laws while employed at the National Institutes of Health. The U.S. Justice Department alleges that he concealed or destroyed records of discussions related to COVID-19 research grants, including an effort to revive a controversial coronavirus grant.

items.title: He's one reason why it's taking so long for the Gordie Howe bridge to open
items.summary_auto: Phil Saoud is an engineer whose consulting firm is responsible for making sure every system on the span works properly and safely. The team is one of the reasons behind why the new crossing set to link Windsor, Ont., and Detroit has yet to open.

items.title: Ontario considering social media, cellphone ban on school properties
items.summary_auto: Ontario Education Minister Paul Calandra says the province will be working closely with the federal government on a social media ban for kids under a certain age. He also says he is considering implementing an "outright ban" of cellphones on school properties, with some medical exemptions.

items.title: 1 woman, 5 pairs of heels and a 3,000-km journey shed light on human trafficking
items.summary_auto: Dora Alcover walked from Spain to Romania to turn the spotlight on sex trafficking. Montreal advocates say they welcome her initiative, adding that it’s drawing attention to the issue here in Canada.

items.title: Boy, 17, charged with animal cruelty after Canada geese driven over and killed in Winnipeg
items.summary_auto: Animal cruelty charges have been laid against a 17-year-old after he allegedly drove a vehicle over several sleeping geese in a Winnipeg parking lot, killing most of them.

items.title: Toronto resale tickets for World Cup no longer on FIFA site following new price cap rule
items.summary_auto: FIFA's marketplace for resale World Cup tickets no longer has seats listed for Toronto matches after a new price cap came into effect last week. The Ontario legislation bans people and platforms from reselling seats to events in the province for more than face value.

items.title: Phillies fire Canadian manager Rob Thomson following 11th loss in 12 games
items.summary_auto: Canadian manager Rob Thomson, who led the Phillies to four straight playoff appearances, including the 2022 World Series, was fired on Tuesday after Philadelphia lost 11 of 12 games and began the day tied for last place in the majors.

items.title: Overland flooding triggers evacuation alert in community southeast of Fort McMurray
items.summary_auto: A northern Alberta community southeast of Fort McMurray has been placed on evacuation alert as the thawing Clearwater River begins to overflow its banks.

items.title: Man pleads guilty in 2002 killing of Run-DMC's Jam Master Jay
items.summary_auto: Several decades after rap star Jam Master Jay of Run-DMC was shot to death, a man named Jay Bryant pleaded guilty to a federal murder charge, telling a judge he helped other people get into a recording studio to ambush the DJ.

items.title: Police identify 18-year-old accused of 1st-degree murder in Windsor, Ont., stabbing
items.summary_auto: A 16-year-old boy was killed Sunday night at a plaza in the city.

items.title: U.A.E. leaving OPEC amid Middle East energy supply crunch
items.summary_auto: The loss of the U.A.E., a longstanding OPEC member, could weaken the group, which has usually sought to show a ​united front — despite internal disagreements over a range of issues, from geopolitics to production quotas.

items.title: Boy dead after fall from downtown Ottawa highrise, first responders say
items.summary_auto: Ottawa police and paramedics say a young boy has died after falling from an apartment window on Laurier Avenue E. around the University of Ottawa Monday evening.

items.title: Support for Alberta separatism flat, struggling high earners more likely supporters: poll
items.summary_auto: After months of signature-gathering and with a potential referendum on independence on the horizon, a new poll done for CBC News suggests Albertans' support for separatism remains "baked in," with few persuaded to change sides.

items.title: These B.C. kids spend their days greeting train passengers, but beloved tradition is in jeopardy
items.summary_auto: A group of children in Pemberton, B.C., has been welcoming visitors to their town for as long as they can remember. For six months of the year, twice a week, every week, they wait for the train to arrive and greet people.

items.title: Austrian citizen pleads guilty to Taylor Swift concert attack plot
items.summary_auto: A man accused of pledging allegiance to the Islamic State group and plotting to attack one of superstar singer Taylor Swift's concerts in Vienna nearly two years ago pleaded guilty as his trial began on Tuesday, Austria media reported.

items.title: Train collision in Indonesia kills 15, injures dozens as rescuers recover last victims
items.summary_auto: Rescuers finished removing victims from a damaged commuter train car on Tuesday, confirming that the crash outside Indonesia's capital killed 15 people, all of whom were women.

items.title: N.L. municipal candidate with criminal background vows to continue campaign
items.summary_auto: A candidate in the upcoming special election in the Town of Victoria, N.L. says he's "done nothing wrong" and is vowing to continue his campaign, despite an extensive criminal history and an upcoming court date.

items.title: How some Toronto homeowners are protecting themselves amid rise of home invasions
items.summary_auto: Security film, which strengthens window glass making it harder to break, is just one of a number of steps Toronto homeowners have been taking to make their houses more difficult to break into, following a rise in brazen home invasions in the last few years in the city.

items.title: Toronto woman who wants to die says federal MAID committee isn't listening
items.summary_auto: Claire Elyse Brosseau wants to end her life, but under current Canadian legislation she’s not eligible for medical assistance in dying because she only suffers from mental illnesses. Next year, that’s supposed to change — though the federal government has already delayed twice.

items.title: Canada is getting a sovereign wealth fund. What are they and how might this one work?
items.summary_auto: Sovereign wealth funds are usually started with extra money that governments then use to invest. But the Canada Strong Fund will differ from wealth funds in some other countries, and there are plenty of details that still need to be sorted out.

items.title: Liberals promising improved bottom line in today's spring economic update
items.summary_auto: The federal Liberals will table their spring economic update today, with Prime Minister Mark Carney hinting at a rosier bottom line with smaller-than-forecasted deficits.

items.title: Colleagues describe Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil's final hours as Israeli bombs rained down
items.summary_auto: An Israeli airstrike killed Amal Khalil on Wednesday and injured her colleague in the south of Lebanon. The journalist's colleagues accuse Israel of targeting her deliberately and obstructing first responders from reaching her in time to save her life, which Israel denies.

items.title: Shell invests $22 billion in Canada's oilpatch and more deals could be coming
items.summary_auto: After selling off a large chunk of its Canadian assets nearly a decade ago, U.K.-based energy giant Shell is now expanding its operations in Alberta and British Columbia by acquiring Calgary-based ARC Resources for $22 billion.

items.title: Vancouver Goldeneyes' debut season scores major victory with fans
items.summary_auto: They may not have made the playoffs but the Goldeneyes were first in one category: PWHL regular-season attendance.

items.title: B.C. officers now allowed to kill abandoned domestic sheep to protect wild animals
items.summary_auto: In a statement, the province says it has changed how domestic sheep are classified under the Wildlife Act in order to prevent disease from being transmitted in what could be large-scale die-offs in wild herds.

items.title: Conspiracy theories abound in aftermath of White House correspondents' dinner shooting
items.summary_auto: A tidal wave of conspiracy theories hit social media as soon as news broke that shots were fired at the Washington Hilton hotel during the White House Correspondents' dinner Saturday. That was to be expected, but some say the dynamics are shifting, with theories coming from liberals and progressives as well as right-wing accounts that are critical of U.S. President Donald Trump.

items.title: Governor General visits Tumbler Ridge to check on grieving community
items.summary_auto: Gov. Gen. Mary Simon visited the northeast B.C. community of Tumbler Ridge on Monday as residents continue to grieve the aftermath of a mass shooting on Feb. 10 in which eight people were killed.

items.title: Ontario Provincial Police officer killed in motorcycle crash on Highway 401 in Cobourg, Ont.
items.summary_auto: An Ontario Provincial Police officer was killed in a single-vehicle motorcycle crash on Highway 401 in Cobourg, Ont., on Monday, the OPP says.

items.title: Dances with Wolves actor Nathan Chasing Horse handed life sentence for sexual assaults
items.summary_auto: A Nevada judge sentenced Dances With Wolves actor Nathan Chasing Horse on Monday to life in prison for sexually assaulting Indigenous women and girls.

items.title: Ex-Mountie's lawyer claims no evidence showing he'd threatened B.C. fraudster on China's behalf
items.summary_auto: The Crown has failed to prove William Majcher was taking steps to threaten an alleged B.C.-based fraudster at the behest of the Chinese government, a lawyer for the former Mountie told the judge overseeing Majcher's foreign influence trial Monday.
